title: alsine tenuifolia var. laxa
intro: Alsine tenuifolia var. laxa, also known as creeping cinquefoil or less commonly, Alsine tenuifolia, is a perennial herb belonging to the pink family (Caryophyllaceae) that is native to the Northern Hemisphere. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Alsine tenuifolia var. laxa include its ability to support the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
